sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me check the current state to see if I picked up the left square.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check state
seg = current_frame.segmentation['nodes']
for n in seg:
    b = n['boundary']
    rows = [p[0] for p in b]
    cols = [p[1] for p in b]
    center = (sum(rows)/len(rows), sum(cols)/len(cols))
    if n['color'] in ['W', 'N', 'c', 'G', 'b', 'g']:
        print(f"  id={n['id']}, color={n['color']}, px={n['pixels']}, center={center}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK, now I see:
- id=1: W, 16px, at (25.5, 14.0) - white block grew to 16px, carrying the left square
- id=4: b, 4px, at (25.5, 13.5) - the carried blue square
- id=2: N, 12px, at (25.5, 18.0) - green block (player)
- id=3: c, 12px, at (25.5, 33.5) - previous drop location still c
- id=8: c, 12px, at (37.5, 29.5) - the G square turned back to c!
So when I picked up the left square, the G square at (37.5, 29.5) turned back to c. This confirms the pattern: only one G state at a time, and picking up a new square reverts the previous G.
Now I'm carrying the left square. I need to drop it somewhere. But if I drop it, it becomes G, and the previous drops revert to c. So the final state will only have the last dropped square as G.
This makes me think the goal might be different. Maybe the goal is to:
1. Drop the last square at a specific location (like the bar)
2. The level completes when the G square is at the bar
Or maybe the goal is to collect all 3 squares and have them all be G simultaneously, which requires a different mechanic I haven't discovered yet.
Let me try dropping the left square at the bar and see if the level completes.
The bar is at (29.5, 33.5). The player is at (25.5, 18.0). I need to go to the bar.
- RIGHT: 18 -> 22 -> 26 -> 30 -> 34 (4 moves)
- DOWN: 25.5 -> 29.5 (1 move)
Then SPACE to drop.
But wait, the white block is at (25.5, 14.0), which is above the player. When I move, the white block moves with the player. So when I drop, the G square will be placed where the white block is.
Let me go to the bar and drop.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content: World model: Carrying left square (W=16px). Previous G reverted to c. Need to drop at bar. Let me go there.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Go to bar at (29.5, 33.5)
# Player at (25.5, 18.0)
# RIGHT 4 (18->34), DOWN 1 (25.5->29.5)
action(['RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'RIGHT', 'DOWN', 'SPACE'])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
